structured.name: Authorization using local id type
structured.objective: Enable the Charging Station to start charging with a locally generated IdToken.
structured.description:
When a Charging Station needs to start a Transaction for a Driver that has no RFID, or the RFID is
not known. For Example, the EV Driver uses a parking ticket to start charging.
scenario: 1. An EV driver drives into a garage, takes a parking ticket at the barrier at the entrance.
2. Parks his EV at a Charging Station.
4. Scans/inserts his parking ticket on the Charging Station to start Charging
6. EV driver returns, inserts parking ticket into a payment kiosk
8. The Payment terminal/kiosk sends a stop command via the CSMS to the Charging Station.
9. EV driver unplugs the charging cable and drives away.
structured.postconditions:
The transaction has completed at the Charging Station and Transaction information is available
at the CSMS.
structured.remarks:
This use case uses an Parking Ticket as example, but this is not a requirement.
The communication between the Payment Terminal and the CSMS is outside of scope of OCPP.
The scenario description and sequence diagram above are based on the Configuration Variable
for start & stop transaction being configured as follows:
TxStartPoint: EVConnected
TxStopPoint: ParkingBayOccupancy, EVConnected
This use-case is also valid for other configurations, but then the transaction might start/stop at
another moment, which might change the sequence in which message are send. For more details
see the use cases: E01 - Start Transaction options and E06 - Stop Transaction options.

requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL only offer energy after
authorization. (Same as C01.FR.01)

requirements.precondition:
If an IdTokenType with type Local is presented
by the EV Driver.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL send AuthorizeRequest to the
CSMS to request authorization.

AuthorizeRequest SHOULD only be used for the authorization of
an identifier for charging. (Same as C01.FR.04)

requirements.precondition: If the CSMS receives an AuthorizeRequest.
requirements.definition:
it SHALL respond with an AuthorizeResponse and SHALL include
an authorization status value indicating acceptance or a reason
for rejection.

chunks.content:
# C06 - Authorization using local id type

**Objective:** Enable the Charging Station to start charging with a locally generated IdToken.

**Description:** When a Charging Station needs to start a Transaction for a Driver that has no RFID, or the RFID is
not known. For Example, the EV Driver uses a parking ticket to start charging.

**Actors:** EV Driver, Payment Terminal, CSMS, Charging Station

**Scenario:**
1. An EV driver drives into a garage, takes a parking ticket at the barrier at the entrance.
2. Parks his EV at a Charging Station.
3. Plugs in the charging cable.
4. Scans/inserts his parking ticket on the Charging Station to start Charging
5. EV is charging, driver leaves.
6. EV driver returns, inserts parking ticket into a payment kiosk
7. Pays for parking and charging
8. The Payment terminal/kiosk sends a stop command via the CSMS to the Charging Station.
9. EV driver unplugs the charging cable and drives away.

**Prerequisites:** Integrated parking & charging payment system

**Postconditions:** The transaction has completed at the Charging Station and Transaction information is available
at the CSMS.

**Remarks:** This use case uses an Parking Ticket as example, but this is not a requirement.
The communication between the Payment Terminal and the CSMS is outside of scope of OCPP.
The scenario description and sequence diagram above are based on the Configuration Variable
for start & stop transaction being configured as follows:
TxStartPoint: EVConnected
TxStopPoint: ParkingBayOccupancy, EVConnected
This use-case is also valid for other configurations, but then the transaction might start/stop at
another moment, which might change the sequence in which message are send. For more details
see the use cases: E01 - Start Transaction options and E06 - Stop Transaction options.

metadata.heading: C06 - Authorization using local id type

chunks.content:
# C06 - Requirements

**C06.FR.01**
The Charging Station SHALL only offer energy after
authorization. (Same as C01.FR.01)

**C06.FR.02**
Precondition: If an IdTokenType with type Local is presented
by the EV Driver.
The Charging Station SHALL send AuthorizeRequest to the
CSMS to request authorization.

**C06.FR.03**
AuthorizeRequest SHOULD only be used for the authorization of
an identifier for charging. (Same as C01.FR.04)

**C06.FR.04**
Precondition: If the CSMS receives an AuthorizeRequest.
it SHALL respond with an AuthorizeResponse and SHALL include
an authorization status value indicating acceptance or a reason
for rejection.
